title: Compare encodings as a controlled experiment
description: Benchmark candidate encodings on a fixed split with out-of-fold statistics so the choice is evidence-based, not habit.
body: Use when the right categorical representation for a field is unclear. Avoid defaulting to one-hot everywhere — it is wrong for high-cardinality fields and tree models that handle categories natively. Actions: fix a split, compute each candidate encoding with out-of-fold target statistics where applicable, train the same model on each, and compare validation metrics and dimensionality. Validation signals include a benchmark table ranking encodings and a leakage-free out-of-fold target encoder. Risk boundary: an in-fold target encoding leaks the label; if the benchmark winner used full-set statistics, treat the result as invalid and refit out-of-fold.
